summary: Matched the common fighter aerial attack module by adding src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_AttackAir.c and .h, wiring it into obj_files.mk, deleting most of the corresponding assembly from asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s, and updating Link, GameWatch, Peach, and common attack code to call the newly named ftCo_AttackAir helpers. The PR also renamed the generic early-return macro from GUARD to RETURN_IF across touched IASA/input code, apparently to make control-flow intent clearer for repeated matching patterns.
what_changed: Added a new ftCo_AttackAir.c implementation covering aerial attack input handling, motion-state selection, enter helpers, animation, IASA callbacks for N/F/B/Hi/Lw aerials, physics, and collision.

Added ftCo_AttackAir.h with address-annotated prototypes for ftCo_AttackAir_CheckItemThrowInput, ftCo_AttackAir_GetMsidFromCStick, ftCo_AttackAir_EnterFromCStick, ftCo_AttackAir_EnterFromMsid, ftCo_AttackAir_Anim, each aerial IASA function, Phys, and Coll.
Removed the broad old prototypes ft_8008CE68, ft_8008CFAC, and ft_8008CF70 from ft_0877.h and replaced call sites with named ftCo_AttackAir helpers.
Removed a large block of assembly from asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s corresponding to the now-matched ftCo_AttackAir functions.
Renamed the platform.h macro GUARD(cond) to RETURN_IF(cond), then mechanically updated touched IASA/input routines in common attack, wait, GameWatch attack, Samus special, and related files.
GameWatch aerial code now includes ftCommon/ftCo_AttackAir.h and calls ftCo_AttackAir_GetMsidFromCStick and ftCo_AttackAir_EnterFromMsid instead of unnamed ft_8008CE68/ft_8008CFAC.
Link aerial entry now calls ftCo_AttackAir_EnterFromCStick(gobj) instead of the old unclear ft_8008CF70 placeholder-style symbol.
Peach float attack code, including remaining asm blocks, now references ftCo_AttackAir_GetMsidFromCStick and ftCo_AttackAir_EnterFromMsid by name.
smart_moves: Kept fighter-specific aerial behavior separated in decideFighter: Link/Young Link route to ftLk_AttackAir_Enter, GameWatch routes to ftGw_AttackAirN_DecideAction, and the default path uses the common c-stick entry helper.

Factored aerial motion selection into ftCo_AttackAir_GetMsidFromCStick, capturing the shared logic for c-stick versus l-stick input, neutral deadzones using p_ftCommonData->xDC/xE0, vertical angle threshold p_ftCommonData->x20, and facing-direction-based F/B selection.
Introduced ftCo_AttackAir_EnterFromMsid as the shared motion-state entry point, resetting allow_interrupt, cmd_vars[0], and throw_flags.flags before Fighter_ChangeMotionState and ftAnim_8006EBA4.
Used a DO_IASA macro for the five nearly identical aerial IASA callbacks, with an explicit comment that an inline function broke inlining.

Preserved matching constraints by keeping both a static checkItemThrowInput and exported ftCo_AttackAir_CheckItemThrowInput, documenting that the exported version is identical but uses too much stack for ftCo_AttackAirLw_IASA to call directly.
Used a MUST_MATCH-only unused stack array in ftCo_AttackAir_CheckItemThrowInput to satisfy stack layout without imposing that artifact outside matching builds.
Renamed GUARD to RETURN_IF, making repeated IASA short-circuit chains more self-describing while retaining the compact macro pattern needed by many action checks.
decomp_lessons: When several motion-state callbacks share identical IASA logic, a macro may be the correct matching tool if an inline function changes inlining or codegen.
Do not force one helper into every call site if stack layout differs: duplicating a small function body can be justified when one caller needs lower stack usage for a match.
Migrating from address-style symbols to action-specific names should be paired with header cleanup and caller updates, not just renaming in one C file.
Common fighter action modules often still need character-specific dispatch; matching common code may require preserving special cases for characters like Link and GameWatch.
For aerial attack direction selection, matching code can depend on exact comparisons against common data thresholds and on using unsigned range checks such as motion_id - ftCo_MS_AttackAirN <= 1.
Remaining asm call sites can still be improved by renaming branch targets/callee symbols to the new C function names, improving searchability before full decompilation.
naming_conventions: Use ftCo_AttackAir_* for common aerial attack helpers instead of anonymous address names.
Aerial IASA callbacks follow the directional suffix pattern ftCo_AttackAirN_IASA, ftCo_AttackAirF_IASA, ftCo_AttackAirB_IASA, ftCo_AttackAirHi_IASA, and ftCo_AttackAirLw_IASA.
The new header uses the address-derived include guard GALE01_08CD68 and address comments on prototypes, matching the style used in this codebase for newly split decomp headers.
The generic short-circuit macro name RETURN_IF better describes the behavior than GUARD for IASA/input check chains.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Deleted the matched ftCo_AttackAir assembly range from asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s and replaced it with compiled C in obj_files.mk.
Used DO_IASA as a macro rather than an inline helper because the PR comment says an inline function breaks inlining.
Used RETURN_IF chains to express early-return action-priority checks while preserving compact matching control flow.
Duplicated checkItemThrowInput logic to avoid stack-size mismatch in ftCo_AttackAirLw_IASA.
Included a MUST_MATCH-only unused stack allocation in ftCo_AttackAir_CheckItemThrowInput for exact stack matching.
Preserved partially asm Peach float attack code while updating embedded call labels to the newly named ftCo_AttackAir functions.
Used ABS and common-data thresholds directly in ftCo_AttackAir_GetMsidFromCStick to match the neutral/vertical/horizontal motion-id decision tree.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that remove the duplicated checkItemThrowInput or resolve the stack-size TODO in ftCo_AttackAir.
Search for remaining ft_8008CE68, ft_8008CF70, and ft_8008CFAC references after this PR.
Look for later renames of ftCo_800C3B10, ftCo_800D705C, and ftCo_800D7100 used in the DO_IASA chain.
Investigate whether GUARD was fully replaced by RETURN_IF repository-wide or only in touched files.
Find follow-up decompilation of Peach float attack asm blocks that now call ftCo_AttackAir_GetMsidFromCStick and ftCo_AttackAir_EnterFromMsid.
